back_translation: When there is a dispute between men, and they come near to the judgment, and they judge them, and they justify the righteous and condemn the wicked,
then it shall be, if a son of beating is the wicked one, the judge shall make him lie down and beat him before him according to his wickedness in number.
Forty he shall beat him; he shall not add, lest he add to beat him beyond these many blows, and your brother be degraded in your eyes.
You shall not muzzle an ox in its threshing.

When brothers dwell together, and one of them dies and a son he has not, the wife of the dead shall not be outside to a strange man; her brother-in-law shall come upon her and take her to himself as a wife and do the duty of brother-in-law for her.
And it shall be, the firstborn whom she bears shall rise upon the name of his dead brother, and his name shall not be blotted out from Israel.

But if the man does not desire to take his sister-in-law, then his sister-in-law shall go up to the gate to the elders and she shall say: My brother-in-law refuses to raise up for his brother a name in Israel; he is not willing to act as brother-in-law for me.
Then the elders of his city shall call him, and they shall speak to him, and he shall stand and say: I do not desire to take her.

Then his sister-in-law shall draw near to him before the eyes of the elders, and she shall remove his sandal from upon his foot, and she shall spit in his face, and she shall answer and say: Thus shall be done to the man who will not build the house of his brother.
And his name shall be called in Israel: The house of the unsandaled.
When men struggle together, one and his brother, and the wife of the one comes near to rescue her husband from the hand of the one striking him, and she sends out her hand and seizes his genitals,
then you shall cut off her hand; your eye shall not pity.
There shall not be to you in your bag stone and stone, large and small.
There shall not be to you in your house ephah and ephah, large and small.
A perfect and righteous stone shall be to you; a perfect and righteous ephah shall be to you, so that they may prolong your days upon the ground that the Lord your God is giving to you.
For an abomination of the Lord your God is everyone who does these things, everyone who does injustice.
Remember what Amalek did to you on the way when you came out of Egypt,
how he met you on the way and cut off the tail in you, all the weak ones behind you, and you were tired and weary, and he did not fear God.

And it shall be, when the Lord your God gives you rest from all your enemies around about, in the land that the Lord your God is giving to you as an inheritance to possess, you shall blot out the memory of Amalek from under the heavens; you shall not forget.
